response.justification: Candidate is fundamentally misaligned with role requirements. While a skilled full-stack developer with strong software engineering practices, the resume shows only ~6 months of explicit C++ work versus the required 5–10 years, no Windows platform experience, minimal SQL expertise, and zero Capital Markets or Fixed Income domain knowledge. The professional trajectory focuses on web/backend TypeScript development, not systems-level C++ in financial services.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship with strong TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development practices across multiple roles
Broad full-stack experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and systems programming shows technical adaptability and depth
Excellent communication skills evidenced by multiple talks, workshops, and courses teaching software engineering concepts
concerns: Only ~6 months of explicit C++ experience at National Instruments versus mandatory 5–10 years; most professional work in TypeScript, Node.js, and other languages
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle expertise; resume lists SQL generically with no database-driven development projects mentioned
Zero Capital Markets, Fixed Income, Derivatives, or Investment Banking domain experience; current and past roles are in web platforms, data integration, and GIS systems

key_factors.factor: C++ depth and Windows platform expertise
Capital Markets / Fixed Income domain knowledge
